idea-plugin.name: Jakarta Migration (javax → jakarta)
idea-plugin.description:
Automate Your Java EE to Jakarta EE Migration

Migrate from Java EE 8 (javax.*) to Jakarta EE 9+ seamlessly. This plugin analyzes dependencies, detects migration blockers, and automates refactoring with OpenRewrite.

Why Jakarta EE?

After Oracle donated Java EE to the Eclipse Foundation, the javax.* namespace became jakarta.*. If your code uses old appservers which depend on Java EE, you need this migration in order to upgrade.

Key Features

  • - Risk Estimate - Estimate the risk and effort of migrating
  • - Dependency Scans - Detect which dependencies are Jakarta EE compatible
  • - Dependency Recommendations - Find Jakarta-compatible versions
  • - Platform Scans - Detect frameworks and appservers which need to be upgraded
  • - Source Scans - Detect all code lines which need to be refactored
  • - Migration Strategy - Compare strengths and weaknesses of different migration strategies
  • - AI-Powered - MCP tools for Jetbrains AI Assistant
  • - Dependency Graphs - Visualize your module dependencies
  • - Auto-Refactoring - OpenRewrite-powered refactor recipes
  • - Undo history - Undo previously applied refactors

Experimental Features

  • - Runtime - Analyze runtime errors and suggest fixes
  • - Reports - Print a PDF report of scan results

Supported Technologies

  • Java Versions: Java 11, 17, 21, 25
  • Build Tools: Maven, Gradle
  • Frameworks: Spring Boot 3+, Spring Framework 6+, Jakarta EE 9+
  • Application Servers: Tomcat 10+, WildFly 27+, Jetty 12+, Open Liberty 23+, Payara 7+, JBoss EAP 8+, WebShphere, WebLogic
  • Jakarta Specifications: Jakarta Servlet, JSP, JPA, CDI, Bean Validation, JAX-RS, JAX-WS, JMS, WebSocket, JSON-B, JSON-P

Getting Started

  1. Analyze Your Project: Click "Run Analysis" to scan your project for javax.* dependencies and code
  2. Review Results: Check the Dashboard for migration risks and estimated migration effort
  3. Reports: Print findings in PDF reports
  4. Plan Your Migration: Use the Migration Strategy tab to choose your approach
  5. Refactor Code: Apply OpenRewrite recipes in the Refactor tab
  6. Verify: Re-run analysis to confirm successful migration

Technical Information

  • Architecture: Built on IntelliJ Platform with OpenRewrite for code transformations
  • Analysis: Static analysis of dependencies, source code, and configuration files
  • Compatibility: Works with IntelliJ IDEA 2023.3+ (Community & Ultimate)
  • AI Integration: MCP server for IntelliJ AI Assistant

Free vs Premium

  • Free:Migration risk analysis, dependency scans, version recommendations, migration strategy options
  • Premium: One-click refactoring, platform detection, advanced scans, PDF reports, AI migration assistance

action.description: Analyze and migrate to Jakarta EE
Analyze project with Jakarta Migration AI Assistant

idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.12 - 2026-04-09
  • Removed free trial period
  • Limited scan results to 10 for free verison
  • Limited refactor actions to 10 for free verison
1.0.11 - 2026-04-08
  • Fixed memory leaks when scanning large projects
  • Re-organized main dashboard scan results summary
  • Major code refactor and simplification
  • Added license expiry notification
1.0.10 - 2026-04-06
  • Added risk score dial and estimated migration time
  • Fixed memory leaks when scanning large projects
  • Fixed recommendation of jakarta compatible upgrades
  • Major platform detection and integration testing overhaul
1.0.9 - 2026-04-02
  • Added: Advanced scan for reflection uses of javax packages - detects and analyzes dynamic class loading and reflection calls that reference javax.* packages
  • Added: Much more robust testing of all scans and refactor recipes - comprehensive test coverage ensuring reliability of migration analysis and refactoring functionality
  • Fixed: Corrected Jetbrains marketplace monetisation properties in plugin.xml
  • Added: Complete JetBrains Marketplace validation test suite with JUnit 5
  • Added: Enhanced error handling and test coverage
  • Updated AI assistant MCP tools to support all scan types, risk analysis, refactor history, and PDF reports
1.0.8 - 2026-03-28
  • Major Feature: PDF Reports - Generate comprehensive migration analysis reports in PDF format with detailed findings, recommendations, and visual charts for stakeholder presentations
  • Added: Reports Tab - New dedicated tab in the migration tool window for generating and managing PDF reports with customizable options
  • Added: Report Templates - Multiple report templates including Executive Summary, Technical Analysis, and Migration Roadmap with different detail levels
  • Added: Visual Charts - Interactive charts and graphs showing migration complexity, risk assessment, and dependency analysis in PDF reports
  • Added: Export Options - Save reports to custom locations with automatic timestamping and version control integration
  • Added: Report Preview - Live preview of PDF reports before generation with real-time updates as analysis data changes
  • Enhanced: Dashboard UI - Improved dashboard layout with better visual hierarchy and enhanced data presentation
  • Enhanced: Migration Phases - More detailed phase descriptions with clear action items and timeline estimates
  • Enhanced: Dependency Visualization - Improved dependency graph rendering with better layout algorithms and interactive features
  • Enhanced: Advanced Scans UI - Refined advanced scanning interface with better categorization and filtering options
  • Improved: Tool Window Layout - Reorganized tool window tabs for better workflow and navigation
  • Improved: Status Indicators - Clear visual indicators for analysis progress and completion status
  • Fixed: UI Responsiveness - Resolved performance issues with large project analysis and UI updates
  • Fixed: Tab Switching - Improved tab switching performance and state management
  • Documentation: Updated user guide with PDF reporting workflow and best practices
1.0.7 - 2026-03-21
  • Added: Dynamic Recipe Loading - Advanced Scans tab now dynamically creates UI tabs based on recipe categories from recipes.yaml, eliminating hardcoded tabs and ensuring automatic UI synchronization when new refactoring recipes are added
  • Enhanced: Recipe Service Integration - Improved recipe loading mechanism with better category filtering and dynamic table management
  • Improved: Premium Feature Architecture - Maintained premium license enforcement while implementing dynamic UI structure
  • Added: 100+ Jakarta Migration Recipes - Comprehensive collection of javax → jakarta OpenRewrite recipes covering all major Jakarta EE APIs (Servlet, JPA, Bean Validation, CDI, EJB, JSF, JAX-RS, JAX-WS, JMS, JSON-P, JSON-B, Security, XML, Enterprise, Configuration Files, Annotations)
  • Added: Risk Scoring Tests - Comprehensive test suite with 19 test cases for dashboard risk score calculation with weighted inputs from YAML configuration
  • Fixed: Gradle Configuration - Resolved malformed Java home path causing build failures
  • Fixed: Build System - Resolved Java 21/Gradle compatibility issues for reliable builds
1.0.6 - 2026-03-15
  • Fixed: OpenRewrite recipe class name fix - JavaxValidationToJakartaValidation now correctly maps to JavaxValidationMigrationToJakartaValidation
  • Fixed: RecipeSeeder now loads all 33 recipes from recipes.yaml
  • Fixed: Recipe database now uses recipe name as PRIMARY KEY for stable identity
  • Fixed: Support tab now shows dynamic build timestamp instead of hardcoded old date
  • Added: Recipe validation tests verify all recipes exist and are correctly configured
  • Added: Dynamic build timestamp generation in IntelliJ plugin
  • Added: Support URLs now loaded from support-urls.properties
  • Changed: RecipeSeeder now loads from recipes.yaml for centralized configuration
1.0.5 - 2026-03-10
  • Resolved critical Jackson ClassCastException affecting plugin stability
  • Implemented complete recipe isolation for reliable execution
  • Enhanced fallback mechanisms for non-Maven/Gradle projects
  • Complete OpenRewrite execution isolation
  • Safely isolated recipe execution environment
  • Reflection-based isolated recipe execution
1.0.4 - 2026-03-05
  • Reimplemented core refactoring system with cross-platform fixes and SQLite persistence improvements
1.0.3 - 2026-03-01
  • Fixed Strategy tab - Replaced HYBRID with STRANGLER strategy, now shows 6 strategies
  • Fixed dependency graph colors - Better visual consistency with dependency list
  • Fixed Advanced Scans tab - Proper display when trial starts
  • Fixed bullet list characters - Used proper dash characters in migration strategy tab
  • Added progress bar - Advanced scans progress indicator in dashboard
  • Added History tab - Premium feature for tracking recipe execution history
1.0.2 - 2026-02-25
  • Fixed: 'Total Advanced Issues' now at bottom of scan counts
  • Fixed: Removed 'Jakarta Status Indicator' from dashboard
  • Fixed: 'No Jakarta Support' count now updates correctly after scans
  • Fixed: Advanced scans now hidden until premium/trial activated
  • Added: Premium upgrade banner in Support tab for better visibility
  • Note: Freemium licensing implementation pending - requires JetBrains product parameters and license verification
1.0.1 - 2026-02-20
  • Fixed: Refactor tab now shows actual files changed after applying recipes
  • Fixed: Refactor recipes now properly applied using RefactoringEngine
  • Fixed: Migration strategy phases now have longer, more descriptive text
  • Fixed: JSON deserialization error now shows warning instead of blocking
  • Fixed: Advanced scans tab no longer disappears when switching tabs
  • Fixed: HYBRID strategy replaced with STRANGLER/MICROSERVICES strategy
  • Added: Premium upgrade banner in Support tab for better visibility
  • Improved: Detailed refactoring results showing files processed and changed
1.0.0 - 2026-02-05
  • Initial release to IntelliJ Marketplace
  • Dependency analysis and compatibility detection
  • Module dependency graph visualization
  • Phased migration planning and strategy comparison
  • MCP server integration for AI Assistant
